rationale: Hard pass for Bounce Blast 3D. The "POV: ran home to play Tetris Block Party" hook is a Gen-Z UGC trope aimed at teens/young-20s, which misfits the 25-55 casual relaxer audience, and it explicitly name-drops a competitor archetype (Tetris Block Party) rather than showcasing physics chain-reaction gameplay—the actual differentiator and core loop. Palette (electric purple/yellow/blue) and live-action human framing also clash with the game's dark, calm, abstract 3D aesthetic. Only worth testing if reshot as a lifestyle bumper that transitions into 6+ seconds of authentic gameplay highlighting ball-bounce chain reactions, otherwise skip.
